q:
A cyclist has been hit at an intersection and is lying unconscious on the ground.
Are you allowed to clear the intersection and carefully carry the cyclist to the side of the road?

A. Yes, you must clear the intersection.
B. No, an injured person must remain where they are.
e:
LESSON 29
In an accident with injured persons, you leave the injured where they are and call the emergency services.
If there are injured persons, the police must also come to make the official report.

Are you allowed to clear the intersection and carefully carry the cyclist to the side of the road?
A. Yes, you must clear the intersection.
B. No, an injured person must remain where they are.
clean_e:
LESSON 29
In an accident with injured persons, you leave the injured where they are and call the emergency services.
If there are injured persons, the police must also come to make the official report.

q:
You have driven against a tank car at the back.
There are no injuries, but a chemical substance leaks from the tank car. What do you do?

A. Completing a European collision form is sufficient.
B. Call the number 112.
e:
LESSON 29 - 'Accident'.
Although there are no injuries, call 112, because chemicals can be very dangerous.

q:
You find someone unconscious in a car.
You have switched off the engine, turned on the hazard warning lights and placed the warning triangle.
What should you do next?

A. Carefully remove the person from the vehicle.
B. Check the breathing and start resuscitation if necessary.
C. Call 112.
e:
LESSON 29
- First ensure your own safety and that of others.
- Check the person and call 112 immediately if there is no response.
- Check breathing:
.. Look to see if the chest rises and falls.
.. Listen at the mouth and nose for breathing sounds.
- Is the person breathing?
.. Keep the airway open (tilt the chin upwards).
.. Continue to monitor breathing until emergency services arrive.
- Is the person not breathing or not breathing normally?
..Start resuscitation immediately (chest compressions and, if possible, rescue breaths).
clean_q:
You find someone unconscious in a car.
You have switched off the engine, turned on the hazard warning lights and placed the warning triangle.
What should you do next?
A. Carefully remove the person from the vehicle.
B. Check the breathing and start resuscitation if necessary.
C. Call 112.
clean_e:
LESSON 29
- First ensure your own safety and that of others.
- Check the person and call 112 immediately if there is no response.
- Check breathing:
.. Look to see if the chest rises and falls.
.. Listen at the mouth and nose for breathing sounds.
- Is the person breathing?
.. Keep the airway open (tilt the chin upwards).
.. Continue to monitor breathing until emergency services arrive.
- Is the person not breathing or not breathing normally?
..Start resuscitation immediately (chest compressions and, if possible, rescue breaths).

LESSON 29 - 'Accident'.

the road situation;

q:
When you see these signs in front of a tunnel, you can be sure:

A. That the tunnel is at least 500 meters long.
B. That the tunnel is only 3.5 meters high everywhere.
e:
LESSON 29
The first sign says it is a tunnel that is 500 meters (or longer).
The second sign says your vehicle cannot be higher. The tunnel can be higher in some places.

q:
Should you ask the police to come in case of an accident with only material damage?

A. Yes, always.
B. No, except if the parties do not agree.
C. No, never.
e:
LESSON 29 - 'Accident'.
Always when there are injured.
If there is only material damage, then not. Unless the parties do not agree and do not want to sign the collision form.

q:
You damaged another car while parking. You don’t know who the owner is.
How much time do you have to report it at a police station?

A. 12 hours.
B. 24 hours.
C. As soon as possible.
e:
LESSON 29
If you damage someone’s property and the police cannot come to the scene, you must report it as soon as possible at a police station or online via Police-on-Web (for minor damage). The sooner you report it, the more likely it is that the facts will be correctly recorded. Make sure you have all necessary information, such as the location and time of the incident, the license plate of the damaged vehicle, and photos of the damage.

Make sure you have all necessary information, such as the location and time of the incident, the license plate of the damaged vehicle, and photos of the damage.

q:
To whom must I show my ID card in the event of an accident?

A. To all persons involved in the accident if they ask, and to the qualified officer.
B. Only to the qualified officer.
e:
LESSON 29
In the event of an accident involving only material damage and if you are over 15 years old, you must present your identity card to the other people involved if they request it. In the case of injuries or serious damage, the authorities may also require it.

After which sign do you know that the tunnel has a length of 700 meters?

A. After both signs.
B. After the first sign.
C. After the second sign.
e:
LESSON 29
The first sign indicates that a tunnel of 500 meters starts 700 meters further.
The exact length of the tunnel can be shown on the sign itself. In this case, 700 meters.
